treatment_information.specific_treatment: Bilateral skin sparing mastectomies, left sentinel lymph node biopsy, and right axillary lymph node dissection with lymphaticovenous bypass
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient was well appearing, with incisions healing appropriately and no evidence of hematoma

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with symptoms including an enlarged right axillary lymph node and bloody nipple discharge
Performed image-guided core biopsy of the enlarged right axillary lymph node, positive for metastatic carcinoma, likely of mammary origin
Underwent bilateral MRI revealing enlarged lymph node in the lower right axilla
Underwent second look ultrasound demonstrating a thickened lymph node
Pathology from core biopsy shows metastatic mammary carcinoma
Underwent bilateral skin sparing mastectomies, left sentinel lymph node biopsy, and right axillary lymph node dissection

patient_outcome.final_status: Patient is ambulatory and independent post-surgery with clear mental status.
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Patient has discharge instructions including care for drains and follow-up appointments.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Patient has reported being alert and interactive, indicating stable condition.
